item.question: Which institutions fall directly within the scope of FINMA's circular on video and online identification?
accept: financial intermediaries as defined in Article 2 para. 2 AMLA
financial intermediaries under Art. 2 para. 2 AMLA

item.question: Does FINMA require the audio recording made during a video identification to cover the entire interview rather than only selected parts?
accept: yes, the full duration must be recorded

item.question: A financial intermediary launches the video identification call and only asks the client during the running interview whether they agree to the video identification and audio recording. Is this approach compliant?
accept: no, consent must be obtained before the interview starts

item.question: During video identification, how many security features of the identification document must the financial intermediary examine in addition to reading and decrypting the MRZ?

reason: Question asked for 'which two features' while a gold list element is 'optical security features' — partial answer-token overlap that primes the answer (rule 4). Reworded to 'characteristics'; gold and citations verified correct against Rz 15.

item.question: Besides being official documents issued by the relevant country, which two characteristics must identification documents have in order to be accepted in the video identification process?
accept: holographic-cinematic marks or printed elements with latent image effects

item.question: A bank detects evidence of higher risks in the middle of a video identification interview. May it continue the identification process, provided the client is onboarded only after the approval of a supervisor, a superior or the management board has been granted?
accept: yes, continuation is permitted subject to the required approval
item.section: c) Stopping the video identification process

item.question: A partnership being onboarded via video identification has several authorised representatives, but only some of them are available for the first video interview. May the financial intermediary verify the representatives' identities at different points in time?
accept: yes, the identities need not all be verified simultaneously
item.section: B. Video identification of legal entities or partnerships

reason: Gold and citation verified correct against Rz 28; added accept variants ('simple copy', 'a simple copy', 'simple copy of an ID document') so legitimate shorter formulations of the same legal category score.

item.question: A bank completes a video-based onboarding, but the process cannot fully satisfy all applicable video identification requirements. What evidentiary status do the documents generated electronically during that process then have?
item.gold: simple copy of an identification document
accept: a simple copy of an identification document
equivalent to a simple copy of an identification document
same validity as a simple copy
simple copy of an ID document
item.section: D. Documents equivalent to a simple copy of an identification document for

item.question: When a financial intermediary itself authenticates an electronic copy of an identification document during online identification, how many randomly selected security features must it check at a minimum to assess the document's authenticity?
item.section: B. Online identification by electronic copy of an identification document

item.question: In an online identification where the financial intermediary itself authenticates the electronic copy of the identification document, may it waive the verification payment from the contracting party's bank account if it reads the chip of a biometric identification document and the verified chip data match the information provided and the photograph taken of the contracting party?
accept: yes, the bank transfer may be dispensed with
item.section: B. Online identification by electronic copy of an identification document

reason: Gold list and Rz 34-37.1 citations verified correct and complete for procedure a); added accept variants ('utility invoice', 'mail delivery', 'trustworthy private directory', 'privately managed database or directory') for fair per-item matching.

item.question: In the online identification procedure in which the financial intermediary itself authenticates the electronic copy of the identification document, by which means can the contracting party's residential address be confirmed?
gold: public register or trustworthy private directory
item.section: B. Online identification by electronic copy of an identification document

reason: Gold and citation verified correct against Rz 48; added accept variants including the expanded form 'transaction authentication number' and common 'TAN procedure'/'TAN code' phrasings.

item.question: Instead of a qualified electronic signature, which method can a contracting party use to confirm the electronic declaration of beneficial ownership, provided the method enables reliable attribution to the contracting party?
item.section: B. TAN processes or similar methods
